sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Deportivo Alavés have shown mixed results in their last 5 matches, securing 2 wins and suffering 3 losses, with a narrow 1-0 victory over Real Sociedad as a highlight. Real Madrid, despite a strong position in the league (2nd with 36 points), have been inconsistent lately, with 2 wins, 2 losses, and 1 draw in their last 5 matches, including a surprising 0-2 home loss to Celta Vigo. Their attacking prowess remains evident, though, with 32 goals scored this season compared to Alavés’ 13.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: Real Madrid have dominated recent encounters against Deportivo Alavés, winning all 5 of their last matchups, including the last two head-to-heads (3-2 at home and 0-1 away). Alavés have struggled to keep clean sheets against Madrid, conceding in all 11 of their last meetings, often with over 2.5 goals in these games.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Injuries
sections.text: Real Madrid will miss key forward Kylian Mbappé among other players, which could impact their attacking output despite having other stars like Vinícius Júnior and Jude Bellingham. Alavés, with no reported missing players, will rely on forwards like Lucas Boyé (3 goals) and midfielder Carlos Vicente (3 goals) to exploit any defensive gaps. Madrid’s depth and quality, however, still give them an edge.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics and Tactical Insights
sections.text: Alavés have a solid defensive record, conceding only 15 goals (2nd least in the league), but their attack ranks poorly (17th with 13 goals). Real Madrid’s superior ball possession (though not detailed in stats provided for them) and ability to create big chances (evident from recent high xG games) suggest they’ll likely control the game. Alavés’ high tackle rate (18.1 per game, 2nd in league) could disrupt Madrid’s rhythm.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Real Madrid’s historical dominance over Alavés and their superior league standing, they are favored to win despite recent inconsistent form and absences. Sofascore’s prediction (64% away win) aligns with this, though Alavés’ defensive solidity at home could make it a tight contest. Expect a low-scoring affair with Madrid edging it.

halftimeSections.subtitle: First half overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half at Estadio de Mendizorroza saw Real Madrid take a narrow lead over Deportivo Alavés with a scoreline of 0-1. The sole goal came from Kylian Mbappé in the 24th minute, showcasing Real Madrid's clinical edge. While Alavés held a slight edge in possession and showed determination in the final third, they failed to convert their opportunities into shots on target. Real Madrid, on the other hand, made the most of their limited chances, securing the lead with a well-taken strike.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The momentum in the first half fluctuated significantly between the two sides. Early on, Real Madrid dictated the tempo with a prolonged period of attacking pressure, dominating the game for much of the opening stages. However, Alavés fought back with notable spells of attacking intent, particularly in the latter part of the half, where they consistently pushed forward and created sustained pressure. Despite these efforts, they couldn't break through Real Madrid's defense, while the away side capitalized during a key attacking surge around the time of their goal.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance and statistics
halftimeSections.text: Deportivo Alavés displayed resilience in the first half, holding a slight possession advantage and recording more entries into the final third and penalty area compared to Real Madrid. Their duel-winning stats were also marginally better, indicating a physical presence on the pitch. However, their inability to register a single shot on target highlights a lack of cutting edge in attack. Real Madrid, despite lower possession, were far more effective, with a higher expected goals figure and the solitary shot on target that resulted in the game's only goal so far.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players and contributions
halftimeSections.text: For Real Madrid, Kylian Mbappé stood out with his goal and remains a constant threat, supported by Jude Bellingham's creative input with an assist. Federico Valverde also contributed significantly in midfield with key passes and defensive work. On the Alavés side, players like Antonio Blanco and Calebe Gonçalves showed promise with interceptions and tackles, but the attacking unit, led by Lucas Boyé, struggled to make an impact in front of goal. Abderrahman Rebbach's expected assists suggest some creativity, but it wasn't enough to create clear chances.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half outlook
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead to the second half, Deportivo Alavés are likely to push harder for an equalizer, given their territorial dominance in the latter stages of the first half. They will need to sharpen their finishing to capitalize on their entries into dangerous areas. Real Madrid, with their potent attacking options, might look to exploit counter-attacks, especially through Mbappé and players like Vinícius Júnior, who could come alive in open spaces. Defensive discipline will be crucial for Alavés if they are to avoid conceding again, while Real Madrid may aim to manage the game and protect their lead.
